title: Fermented Wholesome Irish Banana Skillet
description: A light and refreshing breakfast skillet featuring fermented bananas, crispy bacon, and a medley of spices. Perfect for a cozy Irish morning.

ingredients.name: ripe bananas
ingredients.notes: Choose firm, slightly green bananas for optimal fermentation.

ingredients.name: bacon bits
ingredients.notes: Use a small amount for added flavor without overpowering the dish.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Use a gluten-free flour for a kosher-friendly option.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for added texture and flavor.

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use a coarse black pepper for added depth.

ingredients.name: Irish cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: Use a mild cheddar for a creamy texture and flavor.

ingredients.name: fennel fronds
ingredients.notes: Use fresh fennel fronds for added freshness and flavor.

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.notes: Use a small amount for added depth and warmth.

ingredients.name: green onions
ingredients.notes: Use a small amount for added freshness and flavor.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral oil for added moisture.

ingredients.name: apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Use a mild vinegar for added brightness and flavor.

ingredients.name: kombucha SCOBY
ingredients.notes: Use a SCOBY for added probiotics and fermentation.

instructions: Heat the cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the bacon bits and cook until crispy, about 4 minutes.
Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside.
Add the olive oil to the skillet and swirl to coat.
Add the sliced bananas and cook for 3-4 minutes, until slightly caramelized.
Add the flour, salt, black pepper, and a pinch of sugar to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the diced cheese and cook until melted and bubbly, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the chopped green onions, fennel fronds, and ginger to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the bacon to the skillet and stir to combine.
Drizzle the apple cider vinegar over the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the skillet to a wire rack to cool slightly, about 5 minutes.
Slice the skillet into portions and serve immediately.
